factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments.

evidence.text: Increased segment income as a percentage of net sales in all segments due to pricing and productivity improvements.
evidence.subsection: Segment income

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix shifting towards higher-margin segments indicates improved profitability.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating.

evidence.text: The 0.5 percent decrease in consolidated net sales in 2024 from 2023 was primarily the result of decreased sales volume in several businesses.
evidence.subsection: Net sales

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Decelerating organic growth suggests weaker demand or operational challenges.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are mixed, with some declines and increases.

evidence.text: Decreased sales volume in our residential flow and industrial solutions businesses... increased sales volume in our commercial flow business.
evidence.subsection: Net sales

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed unit volume trends with both increases and decreases indicate no clear overall direction.

factors.summary: The Pool segment is growing fastest, while others are stable or declining.

evidence.text: Increased sales volume due to higher demand compared to the prior year; decreased sales volume in our Water Solutions segment.
evidence.subsection: Net sales

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Positive momentum in the Pool segment despite declines in others suggests growth opportunities.

factors.summary: Margins are improving due to pricing and productivity.

evidence.text: The 2.2 percentage point increase in gross profit as a percentage of net sales in 2024 from 2023 was primarily the result of increased selling prices to mitigate impacts of inflationary costs and increased productivity mainly driven by transformation initiatives.
evidence.subsection: Gross profit

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving margins due to pricing and productivity indicate significant positive developments.

factors.summary: Cost reduction initiatives are delivering measurable savings.

evidence.text: Transformation costs of $52.0 million in 2024, compared to $44.3 million in 2023; restructuring costs of $34.4 million in 2024, compared to $9.1 million in 2023.
evidence.subsection: Selling, general and administrative (“SG&A”)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Measurable savings from cost reduction initiatives despite increased costs.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving due to transformation initiatives.

evidence.text: Increased productivity mainly driven by transformation initiatives; 80/20 analysis is expected to create value by focusing on key customers and products.
evidence.subsection: Key trends and uncertainties regarding our existing business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improved productivity from transformation initiatives shows clear positive progress.

factors.summary: Regulatory changes are negatively impacting the company.

evidence.text: The Organization for Economic Co-operation and Development Pillar Two Model Rules... have negatively impacted our effective tax rate in 2024.
evidence.subsection: Key trends and uncertainties regarding our existing business

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Regulatory changes negatively impacting the company highlight significant challenges.

factors.summary: Management is cautious but investing in growth.

evidence.text: Focusing on capital allocation through... accelerating performance with strategically-aligned mergers and acquisitions; focusing growth initiatives that accelerate our investments in digital, innovation, technology and sustainability.
evidence.subsection: Key trends and uncertainties regarding our existing business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management is investing in growth despite caution.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include supply chain pressures and inflation.

evidence.text: Supply chain pressures and inflationary cost increases due to potential tariffs and pressure on global manufacturing to continue into 2025.
evidence.subsection: Key trends and uncertainties regarding our existing business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Ongoing supply chain pressures and inflation pose risks.

factors.summary: Long-term strategy is clear and well-resourced.

evidence.text: Focusing on capital allocation through... accelerating performance with strategically-aligned mergers and acquisitions; focusing growth initiatives that accelerate our investments in digital, innovation, technology and sustainability.
evidence.subsection: Key trends and uncertainties regarding our existing business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Clear and well-resourced long-term strategy.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include commodity prices and tariffs.

evidence.text: The ongoing volatile market for commodities has the potential to continue to drive price increases in our supply chain. In addition, the current U.S. administration has recently implemented tariffs...
evidence.subsection: Key trends and uncertainties regarding our existing business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Commodity price volatility and tariffs are key sensitivities.

factors.summary: Transformation initiatives are a key catalyst.

evidence.text: 80/20 analysis is expected to create value by focusing on key customers and products through quadrant-based strategies. We expect the analysis to result in actions to improve operating performance by driving growth with our highest value customers...
evidence.subsection: Key trends and uncertainties regarding our existing business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Transformation initiatives expected to drive growth.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is improving.

evidence.text: The majority of our backlog is short cycle in nature with shipments within one year from when a customer places an order... improved lead times within each of our reportable segments.
evidence.subsection: BACKLOG OF ORDERS BY SEGMENT

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving capacity utilization supports efficiency.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are normalizing.

evidence.text: The majority of our backlog is short cycle in nature with shipments within one year from when a customer places an order... timing of delivery of orders associated with certain advance sale programs.
evidence.subsection: BACKLOG OF ORDERS BY SEGMENT

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Inventory levels are normalizing without clear trend.

factors.summary: Interest rate impact is neutral.

evidence.text: The 25.1 percent decrease in net interest expense in 2024 from 2023 was the result of: lower variable-rate debt compared to the prior year.
evidence.subsection: Net interest expense / Provision for income taxes

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Interest rate impact is neutral based on evidence.

factors.summary: MA activity is positive.

evidence.text: On December 2, 2024, as part of our Pool reportable segment, we completed the acquisition of G & F Manufacturing... We funded the purchase price for this acquisition with cash on hand.
evidence.subsection: Net sales

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The completion of an acquisition funded with cash indicates positive MA activity.

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are intense.

evidence.text: Supply chain pressures and inflationary cost increases due to potential tariffs and pressure on global manufacturing to continue into 2025.
evidence.subsection: Key trends and uncertainties regarding our existing business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Intense competition and supply chain pressures pose moderate headwinds.

factors.summary: Differentiation is maintained.

evidence.text: We have identified specific product and geographic market opportunities that we find attractive and continue to pursue...
evidence.subsection: Key trends and uncertainties regarding our existing business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Maintaining differentiation through market opportunities is a positive development.

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks such as tariffs, trade restrictions, and international hostilities pose significant challenges.

evidence.text: Changes in U.S. or foreign government administrative policy, including the imposition of, or increases in, tariffs and changes to existing trade agreements, could have a material adverse effect on us.
evidence.subsection: We are exposed to political, regulatory, economic, trade, and other risks that arise from operating a multinational business.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Significant geopolitical risks like tariffs pose major challenges.

factors.summary: Labor relations are generally stable, but there is potential for increased costs due to union negotiations.

evidence.text: Although we believe that our relations with the labor unions and works councils that represent our employees are generally good and we have experienced no material work stoppages recently, we may experience these and other types of conflicts with labor unions, works councils, or other groups representing our employees.
evidence.subsection: Our business may be adversely affected by matters associated with our labor force.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Potential for increased labor costs due to negotiations is a moderate concern.

factors.summary: The company has significant debt, which could harm its financial condition.

evidence.text: As of December 31, 2024, we had $1,663.1 million of total debt outstanding on a consolidated basis.
evidence.subsection: Increased leverage may harm our business, financial condition and results of operations.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: High debt levels pose a significant risk to financial health.

factors.summary: Demand is accelerating in certain segments, though facing challenges due to inflationary pressures and supply chain disruptions.

evidence.text: Increased sales volume due to higher demand compared to the prior year; increased sales due to the acquisition of G & F Manufacturing.
evidence.subsection: Net sales

evidence.text: In recent years, we experienced inflationary cost increases of raw materials, such as metals and resins, drives and motors, as well as increases in logistics, transportation, energy, insurance and labor costs.
evidence.subsection: We may experience cost increases and other inflation.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Demand acceleration is offset by inflation and supply chain issues, leading to mixed signals.

factors.summary: The company's order backlog is currently shrinking, primarily due to the short cycle nature of most orders and timing of deliveries. However, supply chain disruptions and delays pose a risk to the backlog, potentially causing delays in delivering orders or an inability to meet customer demand.

evidence.text: The majority of our backlog is short cycle in nature... The decrease in our overall backlog from the prior year was primarily driven by... timing of delivery of orders associated with certain advance sale programs.
evidence.subsection: BACKLOG OF ORDERS BY SEGMENT

evidence.text: These disruptions or our failure to effectively respond to them may increase product, logistics or labor costs, limit availability of raw materials or cause delays in delivering our backlog, or may cause an inability to deliver products to our customers or meet customer demand.
evidence.subsection: Interruption of our supply chain could affect our ability to produce or deliver our products and could negatively impact our business and profitability.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Shrinking backlog and supply chain risks pose moderate challenges.

factors.summary: The company is actively raising prices across all segments to address inflationary cost increases and mitigate the impacts of inflation. This reflects a proactive approach to managing cost pressures through pricing adjustments.

evidence.text: Increased selling prices across all of our segments to mitigate inflationary cost increases; increased selling prices to mitigate impacts of inflation.
evidence.subsection: Net sales

evidence.text: We strive for productivity improvements and implement increases in selling prices to help mitigate cost increases.
evidence.subsection: We may experience cost increases and other inflation.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is proactively raising prices to address inflationary pressures, indicating a positive strategy to manage costs.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising due to inflationary pressures and supply chain issues, particularly for raw materials such as metals and resins, as well as logistics and transportation costs. While the company is offsetting some of these increases by raising selling prices, there are concerns about the sustainability of these measures given the broad range of cost pressures.

evidence.text: Inflationary cost increases for certain raw materials as well as logistics and transportation costs... increased selling prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases.
evidence.subsection: Key trends and uncertainties regarding our existing business

evidence.text: In recent years, we experienced inflationary cost increases of raw materials, such as metals and resins, drives and motors, as well as increases in logistics, transportation, energy, insurance and labor costs.
evidence.subsection: We may experience cost increases and other inflation.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising input costs due to inflation and supply chain issues pose a challenge, despite efforts to mitigate through price increases.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, creating margin pressure and posing risks to the company's financial performance. These increases include wages, pensions, and healthcare benefits, contributing to broader inflationary pressures.

evidence.text: Inflationary cost increases related to labor costs and certain raw materials.
evidence.subsection: Gross profit

evidence.text: Increases in logistics, transportation, energy, insurance and labor costs (including wages, pensions and health care benefits).
evidence.subsection: We may experience cost increases and other inflation.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Increasing labor costs, including wages and benefits, are creating margin pressure and contributing to inflation.

factors.summary: Trade tariffs are a significant risk with the potential to increase costs and disrupt supply chains. The current U.S. administration's implementation of tariffs and the possibility of additional or increased tariffs contribute to inflationary cost increases.

evidence.text: The current U.S. administration has recently implemented tariffs and has announced the possibility of implementing additional, or increasing current, tariffs; these actions and any reactionary tariff adjustments by other countries may also contribute to inflationary cost increases.
evidence.subsection: Key trends and uncertainties regarding our existing business

evidence.text: Changes in U.S. or foreign government administrative policy, including the imposition of, or increases in, tariffs and changes to existing trade agreements, could have a material adverse effect on us.
evidence.subsection: We are exposed to political, regulatory, economic, trade, and other risks that arise from operating a multinational business.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Trade tariffs are significantly increasing costs and disrupting supply chains, posing a major risk to the company.

factors.summary: The supply chain is facing significant challenges, including ongoing disruptions and increased costs. These issues stem from volatile commodity markets, which have led to price increases, as well as raw material availability and lead time constraints.

evidence.text: Ongoing volatile market for commodities has the potential to continue to drive price increases in our supply chain.
evidence.subsection: Key trends and uncertainties regarding our existing business

evidence.text: In recent years, we experienced supply chain challenges, including increased lead times for raw materials due to availability constraints and high demand for these materials.
evidence.subsection: Interruption of our supply chain could affect our ability to produce or deliver our products and could negatively impact our business and profitability.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Significant supply chain disruptions and cost increases from volatile markets are causing major challenges.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are identified as a headwind, with specific mention of global economic and business challenges, including housing market strength and international hostilities. Additionally, inflation, supply chain pressures, and geopolitical risks are highlighted as contributing factors to cost increases and overall economic uncertainty.

evidence.text: The overall global economic and business conditions impacting our business, including the strength of housing and related markets and conditions relating to international hostilities.
evidence.subsection: Forward-looking statements

evidence.text: The current U.S. administration has recently implemented tariffs and has announced the possibility of implementing additional, or increasing current, tariffs; these actions and any reactionary tariff adjustments by other countries may also contribute to inflationary cost increases.
evidence.subsection: We may experience cost increases and other inflation.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Economic conditions, including inflation and geopolitical risks, are creating a challenging environment for the company.

factors.summary: The company currently has negative FX exposure, as indicated by unfavorable foreign currency effects compared to the prior year. Additionally, foreign exchange movements, particularly the strengthening of the U.S. dollar against the euro, pose a risk of material adverse effects on reported revenue and margins in future periods.

evidence.text: Unfavorable foreign currency effects compared to the prior year; unfavorable foreign currency effects compared to the prior year.
evidence.subsection: Net sales

evidence.text: Fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates, most notably the strengthening of the U.S. dollar against the euro, could have a material adverse effect on our reported revenue in future periods.
evidence.subsection: Seasonality of sales and weather conditions could have a material adverse effect on our financial results.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Negative foreign exchange effects and currency fluctuations pose a significant risk to future revenue and margins.
